title: UK authorities sanction HTX crypto exchange over support for Russia
summary: Formerly Huobi Global, the exchange is the latest entity to be named as part of a crackdown on companies “exploited by Russia to circumvent UK sanctions.”

title: TeraWulf acquires Kentucky AI data center site with planned 1 GW capacity
summary: WULF shares surged on Tuesday after the Bitcoin miner announced its latest move to expand into AI and HPC through a multi-phase buildout through 2030.

title: Gold Declines as Gulf Strikes Cloud Interest Rate Outlook
summary: Gold fell as military strikes in the Persian Gulf dimmed hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that persistent inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

title: US Natural Gas Drops in Thin Trading as Options Contracts Expire
summary: US natural gas futures fell just before options contracts for front-month delivery expired, erasing earlier gains that had been triggered by an increase in domestic gas supplies flowing to liquefied natural gas export terminals on the Gulf Coast. title: Spain Blocks Prediction Markets Kalshi, Polymarket for Potential Gambling Law Violations
summary: Spain is the latest country to clamp down on the rising use of prediction market platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi.

title: AI Chatbots Show Bias Toward Catholicism, Researchers Say
summary: Researchers find AI models steer users toward Catholicism and away from other religions, like Jehovah’s Witnesses.

title: Ethereum, Solana treasury firms Sharplink and Forward join Russell indexes
summary: According to data from earlier this month, about $12.2 trillion in investor assets are benchmarked" to the Russell U.S. indexes.

title: TD Cowen says crypto bill unlikely to pass this year amid worsening political environment
summary: The political environment around the Clarity Act is getting worse, TD Cowen says, making it harder for the crypto bill to pass this year.

summary: Investors should be concerned by Manifold’s ousting, but better to fire too many executives than too few
